verdict: Cost per lead is far above industry norm; the Google Business Profile is doing the heavy lifting.
diagnosis: Spend of $4,994 produced 21 leads at $238 each — well above the roughly $92 industry average for HVAC.

signals.headline: 21 leads cost $4,994 this period
signals.detail: Instagram spend produced only 21 leads. That works out to roughly $238 per lead — well above local home services norms.

signals.headline: 5-star average across 779 reviews
signals.detail: Reputation is a clear strength. Five-star average and steady new reviews give the profile strong trust signals for local searchers.

signals.headline: 49 calls and 68 direction requests
signals.detail: The Google Business Profile drove 49 calls and 68 direction requests. Organic local presence is outperforming paid Instagram on intent.

signals.headline: 1,171 clicks but only 234 link clicks
signals.detail: Most clicks did not reach the website. The gap suggests the ad creative gets taps but fails to push users to take action.

signals.headline: Zero booked revenue tracked this period
signals.detail: Pipeline value sits at $0 with opportunities still open. Either tracking is broken or leads are not being closed promptly.

recommendations.title: Test Google Local Services Ads alongside Instagram
recommendations.detail: Consider shifting part of the Instagram budget to Local Services Ads. Lead costs in that channel typically run $25 to $75, far below the $238 seen here.

recommendations.title: Fix lead follow-up and pipeline tracking
recommendations.detail: Opportunity value sits at $0 with leads still open. Worth auditing speed-to-call and making sure booked jobs flow back into the CRM as revenue.

recommendations.title: Rework the Instant Form for cooling-season intent
recommendations.detail: Test creative built around emergency AC repair and tune-up offers. Summer urgency tends to lift conversion when the ad matches the homeowner's immediate need.
